run.coverageWindow: 2026-07-16 morning PDT through 2026-07-17 morning PDT, with older items used only as carried-forward ledger context

tierChecks.contract: Daily full scan of new items
tierChecks.notes: APTN, B.C. government releases, AFN, NationTalk search paths, IBF, CCIB, FNFA, FNFMB, FNMPC, Indigenous Clean Energy, Indigenous Energy Monitor, FNTC, NACCA, Nunatsiaq, Ha-Shilth-Sa, Indigenous Watchdog and First Peoples Law were checked for new items. The strongest fresh signals were the Simpcw-Yellowhead consent agreement, Gitanmaax cannabis agreement, AFN ministerial/major-project follow-up and the wildfire emergency-resolution file.

tierChecks.contract: Daily change detection plus Friday deeper rotation
tierChecks.notes: First Peoples Law's July 8-14 report remained the latest visible legal roundup and was used only as discovery. Yellowhead Institute, RRII, Tallgrass/IIPWG, Indigenous Finance Collective, Raven, AFOA/JAM, NATOA, Indigenomics, Tulo, FNIGC, ILI, OKT and Osler were checked for fresh long-form or legal material; no Friday item added a structural insight beyond the live Simpcw agreement.
Substack/commentary sources were checked lightly for B.C. and major-project narrative changes. No material claim was founded on commentary.

Ha-Shilth-Sa, IndigiNews, Anishinabek News, The Eastern Door, Nation Magazine and Cabin Radio were checked for regional material. No new regional article cleared the economic-materiality bar after verification, though wildfire reporting was picked up through APTN and Canadian Press.

Native Nations Institute, Harvard Project on Indigenous Governance and Development, First Nations Development Institute and NDN Collective were change-checked. No new material was used in today's edition.

tierChecks.contract: Search label 'Nations Brief Sources' since prior run
tierChecks.notes: Gmail search returned no messages for label:"Nations Brief Sources" after:2026/7/16 -in:spam -in:trash.

candidateLedger.candidate: B.C. and Simpcw consent-based assessment agreement for Yellowhead copper mine
candidateLedger.verification: B.C. government release plus company project disclosure; framed as process agreement, not approval

candidateLedger.candidate: B.C. and Gitanmaax Band section 119 cannabis agreement
candidateLedger.verification: B.C. government release plus New Relationship Trust Gitksannabis program story

candidateLedger.candidate: AFN final-day ministerial exchange on major projects and consultation
candidateLedger.verification: APTN reporting plus AFN AGA primary event context; final resolution package not yet public

candidateLedger.candidate: Namaygoosisagagun wildfire emergency resolution and evacuation support gap
candidateLedger.verification: APTN reporting from AFN plus Canadian Press/Ontario evacuation-support reporting

candidateLedger.candidate: LNG Canada Phase 2 storage-tank equity option
candidateLedger.verification: Known July 14 file from pilot/source registry; no material July 16-17 change found

candidateLedger.candidate: Tsleil-Waututh Nation Burrard Inlet dredging challenge
candidateLedger.verification: APTN/Canadian Press article; no material new filing found in window

candidateLedger.candidate: B.C. Mount Polley tailings-dam height approval over Xat'sull First Nation concerns
candidateLedger.verification: APTN/Canadian Press article; no material new permit step found in window

candidateLedger.candidate: Qat'muk, Skagit Headwaters and Raush Valley Indigenous-led conservation areas
candidateLedger.verification: The Tyee article with B.C. source link; no material new planning step found in window

candidateLedger.candidate: Métis National Council leadership summit item
candidateLedger.verification: Discovered in broad search; did not clear economic-materiality bar for today's brief
Nunatsiaq/Nunavut scan produced no Inuit economic, ownership, capital, procurement or regulatory item that cleared the 24-hour bar

sourceFailuresAndGaps: Some NationTalk and BCAFN pages were not reliably accessible through direct fetch/search and were recorded as coverage gaps rather than used as source foundations.
The final AFN resolution package was not public during the run, so AFN items were framed through APTN reporting plus event context and labelled reported where appropriate.
No fresh Inuit or Métis economic item cleared both materiality and verification standards in the window.
Friday Tier E long-form sources produced no new structural item; The Long Current was omitted rather than filled.

qualityGates.twoSourceVerificationForConsequentialCla: Applied to Simpcw, Gitanmaax, AFN and wildfire items using primary/government, company, institutional or independent reporting. AFN and wildfire items remain reported where primary documents were incomplete or administrative status details came from reporting.
qualityGates.distinctionsBasedLanguage: First Nations-specific items were not generalized to Inuit or Métis. The absence of Inuit/Métis placements is disclosed in Still Watching.
qualityGates.completedVsProposed: Yellowhead is described as a proposed mine and the agreement as a process/assessment agreement, not approval. Cannabis agreement is described as a signed agreement, with economics undisclosed.
qualityGates.bodySourceChips: None intended; numbered references are used and full descriptive source list is placed at the bottom.
qualityGates.dateIntegrity: Included fresh items are July 16-17, 2026. Older B.C. items are explicitly ledger context.
